channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Chaos in Romania's capital after far-right Calin Georgescu barred from presidential redo
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Chaos broke out in Romania's capital Sunday evening as incensed supporters of the far-right populist Calin Georgescu protested the electoral body's decision to reject his candidacy in a presidential election redo.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and VADIM GHIRDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Honduras holds primaries as voter frustration simmers over security and the economy
item.description: TEGUCIGALPA, Honduras (AP) — Voters in Honduras will select candidates from the three main parties Sunday to compete in November’s general election for the presidency in a country that remains deeply polarized.
item.dc:creator: MARLON GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mark Carney wins Liberal Party nomination to replace Trudeau as Canada's next PM
item.description: Former Goldman Sachs executive Mark Carney won the Liberal Party primary on Sunday and will be appointed Canada's next prime minister after the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's neighboring foreign ministers call for lifting sanctions and reconciliation
item.description: AMMAN, Jordan (AP) — Syria's top diplomat and his counterparts from neighboring countries Sunday called for the lifting of Western-led sanctions on Syria and post-war reconciliation.
item.dc:creator: OMAR AKOUR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian forces walked inside a gas pipeline to strike Ukrainian troops from the rear in Kursk
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Russian special forces walked inside a gas pipeline to strike Ukrainian units from the rear in the Kursk region, Ukraine’s military and Russian war bloggers reported, as Moscow claimed fresh gains in its push to recapture parts of the border province that Kyiv seized in a shock offensi
item.dc:creator: JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chaos in Romania's capital after far-right Calin Georgescu barred from presidential redo
item.description: Chaos broke out in Romania’s capital as supporters of the far-right populist Calin Georgescu protested the electoral body’s decision to reject his candidacy in a presidential election redo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: British PM criticized for blocking bill banning first-cousin marriage amid mounting health concerns
item.description: The left-wing British government is under pressure to outlaw cousin-marriage in the country. A recent bill by a member of the Conservative opposition is being blocked by the ruling Labour party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US orders nonemergency government staff to leave South Sudan as tension grows over fighting
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — The U.S. State Department on Sunday ordered nonemergency government personnel to leave South Sudan’s capital as tension escalates because of fighting in the north.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mother of injured Hamas hostage directs plea to 'every mother in this world' to help secure son's release
item.description: Idit Ohel is fighting for the release of son Alon, who has been held hostage in Gaza since Oct. 7, 2023, and is at risk of going blind from an injury sustained in the Hamas attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: Panama has released dozens of migrants deported from the United States who were held for weeks in a remote camp on the condition that they would leave the Central American nation in 30 days.
item.dc:creator: By Megan Janetsky, Alma Solís And Matías Delacroix, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: International Women's Day protests demand equal rights and an end to discrimination, sexual violence
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Women took to the streets of cities across Europe, Africa, South America and elsewhere to mark International Women’s Day with demands for ending inequality and gender-based violence.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Scottish golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestine group over Gaza stance
item.description: President Trump’s Turnberry golf resort in Scotland was vandalized with graffiti by pro-Palestine activists who are protesting his plans for Gaza and to displace Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare cyclone weakens to a tropical low weather system as it approaches the Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — A tropical cyclone weakened to a tropical low weather system on Saturday as it approached Brisbane, Australia’s third-most populous city, bringing flooding rain that was expected to lash the coastal region for days.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polish leader Tusk plans large-scale military training for all adult males to boost reserves
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said Friday that his government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for every adult male in response to the changing security situation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Huge unexploded World War II-era bomb found in crowded Paris residential area
item.description: A half-ton World War II-era bomb found by workers near train tracks outside of Paris is disrupting train and road travel across the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs not the way Trump should slow fentanyl flow: Bolton
item.description: "American importers pay the tariffs and then do everything they can to pass that cost on to American consumers," John Bolton said.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin says a 2022 Ukrainian decree bans Zelenskyy from talks with Putin
item.description: Russia on Wednesday asked how Ukraine could attend potential talks on ending their three-year war when a Ukrainian decree from 2022 rules out negotiations with President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canada may halt electricity exports to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Premier Doug Ford has threatened to cut off electricity exports to the US if President Trump's tariffs are enacted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation
